skill.name: accessibility-compliance
skill.description: Implement WCAG 2.2 compliant web and mobile interfaces with ARIA patterns, keyboard support, screen reader behavior, and inclusive design checks.
skill.summary: Build and audit accessible interfaces using WCAG 2.2, ARIA, keyboard, screen reader, and mobile accessibility guidance.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, credential, cryptography, reconnaissance, and network indicators, but reviewed evidence shows Markdown code fences, accessibility examples, keyboard handling, and standards links. No prompt injection, executable script, credential access, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The external command alerts are false positives. The cited files contain fenced TypeScript, CSS, Swift, Kotlin, XML, and Markdown examples for accessibility patterns, not Ruby backtick execution or runnable shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are Markdown code fences and UI snippets. They do not invoke a shell, define executable scripts, or process user-controlled command strings.

low_findings.title: Sensitive And Cryptography Findings Are Accessibility Terms
low_findings.description: The sensitive file and weak cryptography alerts are false positives caused by terms such as key, keyboard, MD, and ARIA attributes inside accessibility documentation and UI examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text concerns keyboard events, focus management, and accessibility APIs. No private keys, certificates, hashes, encryption routines, or secret material were present.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Public Accessibility References
low_findings.description: The network alerts are false positives. The URLs are public documentation resources from W3C, platform vendors, React Native, Deque, and accessibility education sites, with no runtime network request code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are Markdown resource lists, not code paths. There is no evidence of outbound requests, credential transfer, tracking, or data exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Accessibility requirements are complex across web, iOS, Android, and assistive technology. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical WCAG 2.2, ARIA, keyboard, and screen reader patterns.
actual_capabilities: Explains WCAG 2.2 A, AA, and AAA success criteria for interface implementation.
Provides ARIA examples for accordions, tabs, menus, comboboxes, dialogs, toolbars, and live regions.
Guides keyboard navigation, focus order, focus trapping, and visible focus states.
Covers screen reader support with semantic HTML, labels, landmarks, announcements, and error summaries.
Includes mobile accessibility guidance for VoiceOver, TalkBack, touch targets, gestures, and text scaling.
Supplies testing checklists for keyboard, screen reader, visual, motor, and mobile accessibility review.
limitations: It does not run automated accessibility scanners or browser tests by itself.
It cannot certify legal compliance without expert review and real user testing.
Examples may need adaptation for a specific framework, component library, or design system.
It focuses on implementation guidance, not full product accessibility program management.

use_cases.title: Review a Product Interface
use_cases.description: Audit a page or component against WCAG 2.2, keyboard access, screen reader behavior, and visible focus requirements.

use_cases.title: Design Inclusive Components
use_cases.description: Plan accessible dialogs, menus, tabs, forms, and live regions before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Improve Mobile Accessibility
use_cases.description: Check iOS and Android experiences for touch target size, VoiceOver, TalkBack, gestures, and dynamic text.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Accessibility Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one page or component.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this interface for basic accessibility issues. Focus on labels, alt text, headings, keyboard access, focus visibility, and color contrast.

prompt_templates.title: Fix a Component Pattern
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate implementation help for a custom control.
prompt_templates.prompt: Make this component accessible. Add the correct semantic HTML, ARIA attributes, keyboard interactions, focus behavior, and screen reader announcements.

prompt_templates.title: WCAG 2.2 AA Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this flow against WCAG 2.2 Level AA. Group issues by success criterion, explain user impact, and recommend practical fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Cross-Platform Accessibility Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for web and mobile teams.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an accessibility implementation plan for this product area across web, iOS, and Android. Include ARIA patterns, keyboard behavior, VoiceOver, TalkBack, text scaling, and test coverage.

output_examples.input: A modal dialog closes only when the user clicks the backdrop.
output: Issue: Keyboard users cannot close the dialog predictably.
Fix: Add Escape handling, a visible close button, focus trapping, and focus return after close.
Test: Open the dialog, tab through controls, press Escape, and confirm focus returns to the trigger.

output_examples.input: A custom tab component uses clickable div elements.
output: Issue: The tabs lack semantic roles and keyboard navigation.
Fix: Use tablist, tab, and tabpanel roles with arrow key movement and selected state.
Test: Navigate with Tab, ArrowLeft, ArrowRight, Home, and End.

output_examples.input: A mobile product card has image, price, rating, and add-to-cart actions.
output: Recommendation: Group related content into one accessible element with a clear label.
Recommendation: Provide custom actions for add to cart and view details.
Recommendation: Verify behavior with VoiceOver and TalkBack touch exploration.

best_practices: Start with semantic HTML or native platform controls before adding ARIA.
Test every interactive flow with keyboard, screen reader, zoom, and reduced motion settings.
Document accessibility decisions in component examples so teams reuse correct patterns.
anti_patterns: Do not hide focus indicators or rely only on pointer interactions.
Do not use ARIA to recreate behavior that native elements already provide.
Do not treat automated scans as a complete accessibility audit.

faq.question: Does this skill support WCAG 2.2?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes WCAG 2.2 guidance, success criteria examples, and Level AA implementation checks.

faq.question: Can it make a product legally compliant?
faq.answer: No. It supports implementation and review, but legal compliance needs expert judgment and real user validation.

faq.question: Does it cover mobile accessibility?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes iOS VoiceOver, Android TalkBack, touch target, gesture, and text scaling guidance.

faq.question: Can it replace automated accessibility tools?
faq.answer: No. Use it with tools such as axe, WAVE, Lighthouse, and manual assistive technology testing.

faq.question: What component patterns are included?
faq.answer: It covers buttons, forms, dialogs, accordions, tabs, menus, comboboxes, toolbars, and live regions.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
